children.heading: Public water systems supervision programs; applications for grants; allotment of sums; waiver of grant restrictions; notice of approval or disapproval of application; authorization of appropriations
children.label: a Public water systems supervision programs; applications for grants; allotment of sums; waiver of grant restrictions; notice of approval or disapproval of application; authorization of appropriations

children.text: From allotments made pursuant to paragraph (4), the Administrator may make grants to States to carry out public water system supervision programs.

children.label: A
children.text: has established or will establish within one year from the date of such grant a public water system supervision program, and

children.label: B
children.text: will, within that one year, assume primary enforcement responsibility for public water systems within the State.

A grant under paragraph (1) shall be made to cover not more than 75 per centum of the grant recipient’s costs (as determined under regulations of the Administrator) in carrying out, during the one-year period beginning on the date the grant is made, a public water system supervision program.

In each fiscal year the Administrator shall, in accordance, with regulations, allot the sums appropriated for such year under paragraph (5) among the States on the basis of population, geographical area, number of public water systems, and other relevant factors. No State shall receive less than 1 per centum of the annual appropriation for grants under paragraph (1): , That the Administrator may, by regulation, reduce such percentage in accordance with the criteria specified in this paragraph: , That such percentage shall not apply to grants allotted to Guam, American Samoa, or the Virgin Islands.ProvidedAnd provided further

children.label: A
children.text: the State is making a diligent effort to assume and maintain primary enforcement responsibility for public water systems within the State;

children.label: B
children.text: the State has made significant progress toward assuming and maintaining such primary enforcement responsibility; and

children.label: C
children.text: there is reason to believe the State will assume such primary enforcement responsibility by .October 1, 1979

children.label: A
children.text: within ninety days after receipt of such application, or

children.label: B
children.text: not later than the first day of the fiscal year for which the grant application is made,

children.heading: .—Authorization
children.label: 7 .—Authorization
children.text: For the purpose of making grants under paragraph (1), there are authorized to be appropriated $125,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2020 and 2021.

children.heading: .—Reservation of funds by the administrator
children.label: 8 .—Reservation of funds by the administrator
children.text: If the Administrator assumes the primary enforcement responsibility of a State public water system supervision program, the Administrator may reserve from funds made available pursuant to this subsection an amount equal to the amount that would otherwise have been provided to the State pursuant to this subsection. The Administrator shall use the funds reserved pursuant to this paragraph to ensure the full and effective administration of a public water system supervision program in the State.

children.heading: .—Reservation of funds
children.label: A .—Reservation of funds
children.text: For any fiscal year for which the amount made available to the Administrator by appropriations to carry out this subsection is less than the amount that the Administrator determines is necessary to supplement funds made available pursuant to paragraph (8) to ensure the full and effective administration of a public water system supervision program in a State, the Administrator may reserve from the funds made available to the State under (relating to State loan funds) an amount that is equal to the amount of the shortfall. This paragraph shall not apply to any State not exercising primary enforcement responsibility for public water systems as of .section 300j–12 of this titleAugust 6, 1996

children.heading: .—Duty of administrator
children.label: B .—Duty of administrator
children.text: If the Administrator reserves funds from the allocation of a State under subparagraph (A), the Administrator shall carry out in the State each of the activities that would be required of the State if the State had primary enforcement authority under .section 300g–2 of this title

children.heading: Underground water source protection programs; applications for grants; allotment of sums; authorization of appropriations
children.label: b Underground water source protection programs; applications for grants; allotment of sums; authorization of appropriations

children.text: From allotments made pursuant to paragraph (4), the Administrator may make grants to States to carry out underground water source protection programs.

No grant may be made under paragraph (1) unless an application therefor has been submitted to the Administrator in such form and manner as he may require. No grant may be made to any State under paragraph (1) unless the State has assumed primary enforcement responsibility within two years after the date the Administrator promulgates regulations for State underground injection control programs under . The prohibition contained in the preceding sentence shall not apply to such grants when made to Indian Tribes.section 300h of this title

A grant under paragraph (1) shall be made to cover not more than 75 per centum of the grant recipient’s cost (as determined under regulations of the Administrator) in carrying out, during the one-year period beginning on the date the grant is made, and underground water source protection program.

In each fiscal year the Administrator shall, in accordance with regulations, allot the sums appropriated for such year under paragraph (5) among the States on the basis of population, geographical area, and other relevant factors.

For purposes of making grants under paragraph (1) there are authorized to be appropriated $5,000,000 for the fiscal year ending , $7,500,000 for the fiscal year ending , $10,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 1978 and 1979, $7,795,000 for the fiscal year ending , $18,000,000 for the fiscal year ending , and $21,000,000 for the fiscal year ending . For the purpose of making grants under paragraph (1) there are authorized to be appropriated not more than the following amounts:June 30, 1976June 30, 1977September 30, 1980September 30, 1981September 30, 1982Fiscal year:Amount1987$19,700,000198819,700,000198920,850,000199020,850,000199120,850,0001992–200315,000,000.

The term “public water system supervision program” means a program for the adoption and enforcement of drinking water regulations (with such variances and exemptions from such regulations under conditions and in a manner which is not less stringent than the conditions under, and the manner in, which variances and exemptions may be granted under sections 300g–4 and 300g–5 of this title) which are no less stringent than the national primary drinking water regulations under , and for keeping records and making reports required by .section 300g–1 of this titlesection 300g–2(a)(3) of this title

The term “underground water source protection program” means a program for the adoption and enforcement of a program which meets the requirements of regulations under , and for keeping records and making reports required by . Such term includes, where applicable, a program which meets the requirements of .section 300h of this titlesection 300h–1(b)(1)(A)(ii) of this titlesection 300h–4 of this title

children.heading: New York City watershed protection program
children.label: d New York City watershed protection program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator is authorized to provide financial assistance to the State of New York for demonstration projects implemented as part of the watershed program for the protection and enhancement of the quality of source waters of the New York City water supply system, including projects that demonstrate, assess, or provide for comprehensive monitoring and surveillance and projects necessary to comply with the criteria for avoiding filtration contained in 40 CFR 141.71. Demonstration projects which shall be eligible for financial assistance shall be certified to the Administrator by the State of New York as satisfying the purposes of this subsection. In certifying projects to the Administrator, the State of New York shall give priority to monitoring projects that have undergone peer review.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 5 years after the date on which the Administrator first provides assistance pursuant to this paragraph, the Governor of the State of New York shall submit a report to the Administrator on the results of projects assisted.

children.heading: Matching requirements
children.label: 3 Matching requirements
children.text: Federal assistance provided under this subsection shall not exceed 50 percent of the total cost of the protection program being carried out for any particular watershed or ground water recharge area.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: 4 Authorization
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Administrator to carry out this subsection for each of fiscal years 2003 through 2010, $15,000,000 for the purpose of providing assistance to the State of New York to carry out paragraph (1).
